channel.title: Ground Truth — GenAI Systems Lab
channel.description: Production AI systems deep dives — RAG, agents, evals, LLMOps, safety, and system design for AI engineers.

item.title: What Is a Transformer? Self-Attention Explained Without the Math
item.description: Why every LLM is built on the same core idea — and what attention actually computes, step by step.

item.title: Self-Attention: From Dot Products to What the Model Focuses On
item.description: Query, Key, Value matrices explained. Why multi-head attention sees different things at once, and what that means for long-range dependencies.

item.title: Tokenization: Why 'cat' and 'cats' Are Different to an LLM
item.description: BPE, WordPiece, SentencePiece — what tokenizers do, why it matters for prompting, and how token counts drive your inference bill.

item.title: Embeddings Explained: How Text Becomes Geometry
item.description: What embedding vectors represent, why semantic similarity works, and how this underpins every RAG system and search product.

item.title: Temperature, Top-P, Top-K: How LLMs Actually Choose the Next Word
item.description: Greedy, beam search, nucleus sampling — what each one does, when randomness helps, and why temperature 0 isn't always the right answer.

item.title: The Context Window: What Goes In, What Gets Dropped, and Why It Matters
item.description: How LLMs process long inputs, what context overflow looks like in production, and strategies to stay within the window without losing what matters.

item.title: Prompt Engineering & Token Economics
item.description: How prompt structure affects quality, why few-shot beats zero-shot in most cases, and how to calculate real inference cost before you ship.

item.title: How RAG Actually Works — And Why It's Harder Than It Looks
item.description: The full retrieval-augmented generation pipeline: chunking → embedding → retrieval → reranking → generation. Where each step silently fails.

item.title: Chunking Strategies for RAG: Fixed, Semantic, and Hierarchical
item.description: Why chunk size is one of the most impactful RAG config decisions. Fixed-size vs. sentence vs. semantic chunking, with real retrieval quality differences.

item.title: RAG Architectures: Naive, Advanced, Modular, and Agentic
item.description: How RAG has evolved from a simple retrieve-and-read loop to routing, query rewriting, self-RAG, corrective RAG, and full agentic retrieval.

item.title: Reranking: Why Top-K Retrieval Isn't Enough
item.description: How cross-encoders and rerankers improve precision after initial retrieval, when the latency cost is worth it, and how to evaluate reranker quality.

item.title: Vector Databases Compared: Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, pgvector
item.description: Latency, scale, metadata filtering, cost, and managed vs. self-hosted tradeoffs. A decision framework for choosing your vector store.

item.title: Missing Context: When RAG Retrieves the Right Chunk but Answers the Wrong Question
item.description: Why high similarity score doesn't mean high relevance. The missing context failure mode, why it's hard to detect, and how to fix it.

item.title: Ambiguous Queries: Why RAG Struggles When the Question Has Two Meanings
item.description: Multi-intent queries, under-specified questions, and how your retriever picks the wrong meaning — and confidently answers it.

item.title: Hybrid Search: Combining BM25 and Vector Retrieval
item.description: Why pure semantic search misses exact matches, and pure keyword search misses meaning. How hybrid search with RRF fusion beats both.

item.title: The ReAct Pattern: How LLM Agents Reason and Act
item.description: Thought → Action → Observation loops explained. How ReAct enables tool use, where it breaks, and how to trace what your agent actually did.

item.title: Tool Use Design for AI Agents: Contracts, Consequences, and MCP
item.description: How to design tools an agent won't misuse. Consequence levels, idempotency, permission architecture, and the Model Context Protocol (MCP).

item.title: 6 Types of Memory in AI Agents (And When to Use Each)
item.description: In-context, episodic, semantic, procedural, working, and external memory — what each stores, how it's retrieved, and real implementation patterns.

item.title: Multi-Agent Orchestration: Supervisor, Pipeline, and Mesh Patterns
item.description: How to break a complex task across multiple agents. Supervisor vs. pipeline vs. mesh patterns, inter-agent communication, and failure budgets.

item.title: Planning Patterns for AI Agents: ToT, GoT, and LATS
item.description: How agents plan ahead: Tree of Thought, Graph of Thought, LATS, and reflection loops. When complex planning beats straight ReAct.

item.title: Tracing Agent Loops: How to Debug Step-by-Step Execution
item.description: What a step trace reveals, how to spot loops, wrong tool calls, and hallucinated observations — and how to use the Agent Loop Simulator to reproduce failures.

item.title: How AI Agents Fail in Production: A Full Taxonomy
item.description: Tool misuse, infinite loops, hallucinated tool calls, context bleed, approval fatigue, compounding reliability failures — with worked examples from the lab.

item.title: How to Evaluate LLM Systems: RAGAS, G-Eval, and Custom Grading
item.description: What groundedness, faithfulness, and citation accuracy actually measure — and how to build an eval pipeline that catches real failures before users do.

item.title: Hallucination Detection: Why It's Hard and What Actually Works
item.description: Factual vs. faithfulness vs. citation hallucinations. NLI-based detection, self-consistency, and retrieval grounding — tested against real examples.

item.title: Building an Eval Pipeline That Actually Catches Production Failures
item.description: Why unit tests aren't enough for LLMs. How to design offline evals, online evals, and shadow evaluation so regressions don't reach users.

item.title: A/B Testing LLM Systems: Statistical Significance and Evaluation Metrics
item.description: How to run controlled experiments on LLM outputs, which metrics to use (win-rate, NDCG, preference), and how to avoid common A/B traps.

item.title: LLMOps: What Production AI Actually Needs That Tutorials Skip
item.description: Observability, prompt versioning, latency budgets, cost tracking, model routers, A/B testing, and rollback strategies. The full production checklist.

item.title: Fine-Tuning vs. RAG vs. Prompt Engineering: When to Use What
item.description: The decision framework every AI engineer needs. Cost, latency, data requirements, update frequency, and failure modes for each approach.

item.title: Model Routing: How to Send the Right Query to the Right Model
item.description: Complexity-based routing, cost-based routing, and capability routing. How model routers cut inference cost by 40-70% without hurting quality.

item.title: LLM Inference Optimisation: Batching, Quantisation, and Speculative Decoding
item.description: How to reduce latency and cost at inference time without retraining. INT8/INT4 quantisation, continuous batching, speculative decoding explained.
